Package neqsim.pvtsimulation.util

Class ZFactorCorrelations

java.lang.Object
neqsim.pvtsimulation.util.ZFactorCorrelations
public final class ZFactorCorrelations extends Object
Standalone Z-factor (gas compressibility factor) correlations.

Provides multiple correlations for calculating the gas compressibility factor (Z) from pseudoreduced temperature and pressure. Also includes convenience methods that accept Kelvin, bara, and gas specific gravity directly.

Default units: All convenience methods use Kelvin and bara. Core correlations (hallYarborough(double, double), dranchukAbouKassem(double, double), papay(double, double)) are dimensionless and take pseudoreduced properties directly.

Correlations:

  • Hall-Yarborough (1973) - Newton-Raphson iterative solution
  • Dranchuk-Abou-Kassem (1975) - 11-constant BWR equation
  • Papay (1968) - simple explicit correlation
Version:
2.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Method Details

    • hallYarborough

      public static double hallYarborough(double tpr, double ppr)
      Hall-Yarborough (1973) Z-factor correlation.

      Iterative Newton-Raphson solution of the Starling-Carnahan equation of state. Uses reduced density y as the primary unknown.

      Parameters:
      tpr - Pseudoreduced temperature (dimensionless). Valid: 1.0 - 3.0
      ppr - Pseudoreduced pressure (dimensionless). Valid: 0 - 25
      Returns:
      Z-factor (dimensionless)

    • dranchukAbouKassem

      public static double dranchukAbouKassem(double tpr, double ppr)
      Dranchuk-Abou-Kassem (1975) Z-factor correlation.

      Eleven-constant modified Benedict-Webb-Rubin equation of state. Uses iterative Newton-Raphson solution for reduced density.

      Parameters:
      tpr - Pseudoreduced temperature (dimensionless). Valid: 1.0 - 3.0
      ppr - Pseudoreduced pressure (dimensionless). Valid: 0.2 - 30
      Returns:
      Z-factor (dimensionless)

    • papay

      public static double papay(double tpr, double ppr)
      Papay (1968) explicit Z-factor correlation.

      Simple explicit formula. Less accurate than iterative methods but useful for quick estimates or as an initial guess. $$ Z = 1 - \frac{3.52 P_{pr}}{10^{0.9813 T_{pr}}} + \frac{0.274 P_{pr}^2}{10^{0.8157 T_{pr}}} $$

      Parameters:
      tpr - Pseudoreduced temperature (dimensionless). Valid: 1.0 - 3.0
      ppr - Pseudoreduced pressure (dimensionless). Valid: 0.2 - 15
      Returns:
      Z-factor (dimensionless)

    • zFactorSutton

      public static double zFactorSutton(double pressureBara, double temperatureK, double gammaG)
      Z-factor using Sutton pseudocritical properties and Hall-Yarborough correlation.
      Parameters:
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara)
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin)
      gammaG - Gas specific gravity (air = 1.0)
      Returns:
      Z-factor (dimensionless)

    • zFactorSourGas

      public static double zFactorSourGas(double pressureBara, double temperatureK, double gammaG, double yH2S, double yCO2)
      Z-factor for sour gas with Wichert-Aziz correction.
      Parameters:
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara)
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin)
      gammaG - Gas specific gravity (air = 1.0)
      yH2S - Mole fraction of H2S [0, 1]
      yCO2 - Mole fraction of CO2 [0, 1]
      Returns:
      Z-factor (dimensionless)

    • gasDensity

      public static double gasDensity(double pressureBara, double temperatureK, double gammaG, double molWeight)
      Gas density using Z-factor from Sutton + Hall-Yarborough.
      Parameters:
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara)
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin)
      gammaG - Gas specific gravity (air = 1.0)
      molWeight - Gas molecular weight (g/mol)
      Returns:
      Gas density (kg/m3)

    • gasFVFFromZ

      public static double gasFVFFromZ(double pressureBara, double temperatureK, double gammaG)
      Gas formation volume factor from Z-factor.

      $$ B_g = \frac{Z \, T}{P} \times \frac{P_{sc}}{T_{sc}} $$ Standard conditions: 1.01325 bara, 288.71 K (15.56 C / 60 F). Returns Bg in reservoir m3 / standard m3.

      Parameters:
      pressureBara - Pressure (bara)
      temperatureK - Temperature (Kelvin)
      gammaG - Gas specific gravity (air = 1.0)
      Returns:
      Gas FVF (res m3 / std m3)

    • compareAll

      public static Map<String,Double> compareAll(double tpr, double ppr)
      Compare all three Z-factor correlations at the given reduced conditions.
      Parameters:
      tpr - Pseudoreduced temperature (dimensionless)
      ppr - Pseudoreduced pressure (dimensionless)
      Returns:
      Map of correlation name to Z value
